clinical_history: 21 year old female with complaint of palpitations. What does this ECG show?
diagnosis:
This ECG shows the classic Wolff-Parkinson-White (WPW) pattern with the triad of a short PR interval, a wide QRS and a delta wave. The delta wave is represented by notching or slurring of the initial portion of the QRS complex due to ventricular pre-excitation. See Case # 218 for a detailed explanation of this finding and its significance. The polarity of the delta wave in this case (negative in V1 and positive laterally) is consistent with a right-sided bypass tract.

The complaint of palpitations in patients with WPW pattern raises a strong possibility of atrio-ventricular reentrant tachycardia (AVRT), a type of paroxysmal supraventricular tachycardia (PSVT). However, the symptom could be due to other mechanisms, related or non-related to the WPW pattern, including AV nodal reentrant tachycardia (AVNRT), atrial fibrillation, sinus tachycardia, etc.
